title: Add labels on containers that are started within pods
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

When I create podman pods, I have very few information in the container directly that this container is part of a pod
Inspecting the pod (not using compat mode) I've a single field (but no name as well)
```
"Pod": "140e94e830b192325d4f6778f2a59de255611f8cd776efd9728af9066e1f7e5d",
```
but then I need to go to list of pods, etc


**What I would like:**

1. Add some annotations with `Labels` on the container

2. Then when displaying info of a container (even using compat mode) I will clearly see that this container is part of a pod and the pod name will be part of the Label

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Great catch, @benoitf!

> Add some annotations with Labels on the container

This would be inconsistent with other Podman-specific fields that we expose via inspect.  I'd prefer making it a real field in the inspect data.

@mheon WDYT?

FYI, compat API won't show these extra fields (only podman API inspect) while labels will be displayed on all inspect tools.
So it might be interesting (not my usecase as I can use Podman API) to expose the grouping of Pods like for Compose with Labels
That sounds good.  If needed, the compat endpoint can add this information to labels.

I'm fine with adding this to Libpod Inspect as a top-level construct.

Should we consider adding it as an environment variable, so the container itself can tell it's part of a pod?

> Should we consider adding it as an environment variable, so the container itself can tell it's part of a pod?

Any host data injected into a container _can_ be useful in an exploit.  While I have a hard time imagining how knowing the pod can help, I'd vote for not injecting it unless urgently needed.
I agree don't inject it into the container environment.  Lots of cases where a privileged container is monitoring a container.  That information might be an additional risk.
@vrothberg / @rhatdan - if you don't mind, I start working on this.

I don't understand why a docker API caller would be interested in pods?
I think this change would cause problems: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15738#issuecomment-1243527931

I could see making these OCI annotations instead of labels. That will keep
them out of the compat API, and the OCI runtime might be interested that
the container is in a pod ( I know kata wants to know this, for example).

On Mon, Sep 12, 2022 at 13:02 Paul Holzinger ***@***.***>
wrote:

> I don't understand why a docker API caller would be interested in pods?
> I think this change would cause problems: #15738 (comment)
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15738#issuecomment-1243527931>
>
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15374#issuecomment-1244030274>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCCOOKCVP4IIZGN7VL3V55O2BANCNFSM565NGPZQ>
> .
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>
@benoitf is it OK if the pod name will be shown in `annotations`? Unfortunately, `annotations` are not available through `compat API`.
for me it's ok as I'm using the LibPod API, I was just thinking for people using pure "Docker tools" inspecting containers on podman. They won't have more details on containers that are part of a pod
Closing as the feature is not needed anymore.
